title: gouldia sandwicensis
intro: Gouldia sandwicensis, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the island of Hawaii. As a medicinal plant, it has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Gouldia sandwicensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, and has also been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
